\chapter*{Conclusion}
% Do not edit
\addcontentsline{toc}{chapter}{Conclusion}
\label{chap:concl}
\markboth{CONCLUSION}{CONCLUSION}
This research aimed to identify the primary macroeconomic, 
structural, and socio-psychological drivers of sales within the 
global video game industry. \\By combining descriptive insights 
with a Generalized Additive Mixed Model (GAMM), this project 
tried to propose a 360-degree evaluation of the market, moving 
beyond the traditional single-variable analyses but always taking
in consideration the non-linear interactions between the 
aforementioned variables.\\
The descriptive analysis revealed consistent growth trends 
across all major markets over the 2014–2024 period, with North 
America and East Asia emerging as the dominant regions in terms 
of absolute sales levels.
The econometric model achieved a robust adjusted $R^2$ of 0.719, 
confirming that the combination of social factors (such as 
internet users and unemployment) and psychological indicators 
captures a substantial portion of sales variability. \\The 
empirical findings show that purchasing power follows a stable 
linear trend, whereas structural and health-related predictors 
operate through complex, non-linear paths. \\It’s important to pay 
attention to the highly significant interaction between temporal 
dynamics and public mental health trends, which confirms that the
role of video games as coping mechanisms in relation to mental 
health is not static, but evolves alongside broader macroeconomic
cycles.\\
This finding is consistent with the evidence documented by 
\cite{gaming_disorder_covid}, which highlighted the increased 
reliance on video games as a coping strategy during periods of 
psychological distress.\\
Our multidimensional approach highlights specific structural 
limitations. \\A notable discrepancy emerged between the descriptive 
evidence — which showed a sharp spike in active players during 
the initial COVID-19 lockdowns — and the non-significance of the 
Stringency Index in the final model. \\This divergence is likely 
attributable to a temporal mismatch: the pandemic restriction 
shock was highly concentrated in early 2020, whereas the index 
measures restrictions along a continuous three-year period, 
smoothing out the short-term spike of consumer reactions.\\ 
Furthermore, exploring the role of economic disparity through 
the Gini Index was ultimately unfeasible due to severe data 
gaps, which would have compromised the model's sample size and 
efficiency.\\
Future extensions of this work should aim to secure more granular
panel data to successfully integrate wealth inequality metrics 
alongside broader socio-economic variables. \\
By proving the profound interplay between structural economics, 
digital evolution, and public well-being, this study establishes 
a solid foundation for understanding the modern entertainment 
economy, offering an optimal starting base for both market 
analysts and future academic research.
